verdict.answer_first: Tie: both answer in sentence 1. A: 'Use a managed provider, not your own.' B: 'use a managed auth provider, don't roll your own.'
verdict.decisiveness: A commits more clearly to a concrete next step: 'start with Clerk or Supabase Auth depending on whether you value speed-to-ship... or low cost and data ownership'; B stays more segmented by cases.
verdict.fabrication: Both make uncited concrete claims; A says 'Both get you to production in a day' and pricing has 'reputation for jumping sharply'; B says Auth0 'gets expensive fast' and pricing has 'history of jumps' without evidence.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it narrows to two choices and gives a decision rule: 'speed-to-ship (Clerk) or low cost and data ownership (Supabase).'
